name: Steve Chabot
subject: So Disappointing
body:
drifting further and further to the left... No images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-z/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-y/)
### [Chief Justice Roberts Disappoints Again](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-j/)
This past week, Chief Justice John Roberts joined the other liberal Supreme Court judges to strike down a Louisiana pro-life law that required abortionists to have admitting privileges in a nearby hospital - another disappointing decision from a Republican appointed Justice. In this week's blog, I discuss the phenomenon of conservative judges trending left. Why do we never see liberal judges going to the right? [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-t/)
# Previous Blog Posts
[](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-i/)
### [Police Reform](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-d/)
Last week, the House Judiciary Committee, which I'm a member, discussed the turmoil in Seattle and the aftermath of the George Floyd killing during our markup on impending police reform legislation. I wanted to use this week's blog to discuss some progress being made on police reform and to give you insight into what I think still needs to be accomplished. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Mob Rule](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-u/)
Mob rule has taken over a major American city. In Seattle, a group of anarchists have taken a police precinct and a considerable portion of the city hostage. In this week's blog, I discuss this radical group's shocking demands which include abolition of the police, courts, and jails, free college, free healthcare, and free housing. How have Seattle's leaders responded to this mob? Will Rule of Law prevail or will this spread to other cities? [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-b/)
### [The Left Now Wants to Abolish the Police](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-n/)
Over the last week, those on the left, and their sympathizers in the mainstream media, have ramped up their radical demand to abolish, defund, dismantle, and even outright ban police departments. In this week's blog, I'll discuss whether reforming law enforcement is warranted. And whether defunding/dismantling police departments is the solution. (Spoiler alert - it's not.) [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-p/) [Donate](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-x/) [Get a Yard Sign](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-m/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-c/) | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-q/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-a/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jktkhjud-kujkulhluu-f/)

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Improving long-term care in Rhode Island
body:
Your RI COVID-19 News Update
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
As we continue our reopening, we have to learn from the challenges we've faced. We can't just rebuild the same long-term care system we started with before COVID-19. We have to evolve to build a safer and more resilient long-term care system - one that provides our loved ones with choices including more access to care options at home, and that re-thinks our approach to congregate living.
Today, I want to talk to you about what we can do right now to improve long term care in Rhode Island and keep our loved ones safe.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001y4ZmOhoyLMWJXgDPzkm_mGYTWkx46heKo23X6jXssnc2JGenNxz54iYr9eo4ryHb0qqjMt3l3OY8R4IZECibylgzl9FurHy_hq18DpJgnAGk36oooM6U8b2S6tUa4DGMYUSQ7DyOEXG3oQdNbebraleNLz0_3nJKHK3tdgVideJgWTK6ayqHkY7EfM3MOcTqQjIRGyqHrqY=&c=L5rHYHbRTa6Lzg0df1ruGrd6UM3FeOyC6lBZG6QUWBUteox8-WscIA==&ch=9HqFGEjkMxRYVR939ALfFdgEObpviBReKfW7w2ylztYYSCOA68eq2g==)
---
| Starting today, we are going to invest more than $25 million in long-term care services that will address immediate needs at nursing facilities and help transform how we deliver care in the long term.
These funds will:
* Provide immediate relief for nursing facilities to support the additional cleaning and PPE needed to keep staff and residents safe.
* Encourage nursing facilities to totally reimagine how they will deliver care in the long term - things like creating single room occupancy and single room bathrooms and specialty units. We want to work with and support nursing facilities to change their business and clinical models as we learn from this crisis.
* Grow and strengthen our home-based care system so Rhode Islanders have a wider array of options for long-term care beyond congregate living. As a state we haven't invested enough in home care and community-based options which can provide older adults more flexibility and independence.

Long-term care planning is stressful. Having an older parent, it's something my siblings and I deal with too. I've often heard from Rhode Islanders who have said they don't know where to look to find home-based options. I am committed to making this easier for you, and this funding we'll help strengthen that system.
We'll be working with hospitals to make sure families receive information about home-based care options when a loved one is being discharged, and we're going to invest further in making information about these options more available to you.
We also have to recognize the long hours for low pay that many of our home care workers endure. Many of these direct care workers are Black or Latino women of color. Making these jobs good paying jobs, with the right training and oversight, will be critical to improving long-term care in Rhode Island. We're going to make targeted investments in home and community-based care workforce supports so that we have safer care and job options over the coming months as we continue to deal with this virus.
We know that this is the start of a long journey. This kind of change can take many years. But we have an opportunity before us today for transformation that will make use safer and more resilient as a state. I want all of us to think creatively and differently about the new long-term care system we will build together, and I want to thank the consumers and industry partners who have been working with my teams at RIDOH and the Executive Office of Health and Human Services to get this effort moving.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001y4ZmOhoyLMWJXgDPzkm_mGYTWkx46heKo23X6jXssnc2JGenNxz54owEk0UzrG5Vt_vAdTMX-nEQOwU0eAFXtu87VNqgSHR2RpZPpiOhOLA3JSM9PWGSBwvPRcic5wXL6Z2CMVF0scOSoXBwLDHjoWNPxgmsYW2jJghNrEUNAouqKcP8fp5egnWB5PPzV8C4dIEXlYEbvBk=&c=L5rHYHbRTa6Lzg0df1ruGrd6UM3FeOyC6lBZG6QUWBUteox8-WscIA==&ch=9HqFGEjkMxRYVR939ALfFdgEObpviBReKfW7w2ylztYYSCOA68eq2g==)
---
| Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's [COVID-19 data dashboard](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001y4ZmOhoyLMWJXgDPzkm_mGYTWkx46heKo23X6jXssnc2JGenNxz54owEk0UzrG5Vt_vAdTMX-nEQOwU0eAFXtu87VNqgSHR2RpZPpiOhOLA3JSM9PWGSBwvPRcic5wXL6Z2CMVF0scOSoXBwLDHjoWNPxgmsYW2jJghNrEUNAouqKcP8fp5egnWB5PPzV8C4dIEXlYEbvBk=&c=L5rHYHbRTa6Lzg0df1ruGrd6UM3FeOyC6lBZG6QUWBUteox8-WscIA==&ch=9HqFGEjkMxRYVR939ALfFdgEObpviBReKfW7w2ylztYYSCOA68eq2g==).
---
| Today was my last press conference for the week, and so I want to share one final thought about the long weekend. I want all Rhode Islanders to enjoy the Fourth of July, but I am concerned that if people don't do so safely, we're going to be in trouble a few weeks from now. In order to help curb the spread, we will be making a one-time distribution of 1,000 masks to every municipality to hand out at gatherings this weekend, and DEM will be distributing masks at parks and beaches. Please, be safe this holiday weekend. We're all in this together.
-Gina Raimondo Governor

| Dear friends, I wanted to take a moment to thank you for your support. This week has been the most important yet on this campaign. We saw record turnout in the Kentucky primary election and won our primary with over 70% of the vote. Then, our team officially closed the books on the second quarter, and we’ll be submitting our report to the FEC shortly. Though we’re still crunching the final numbers, we hit (and passed) every goal we set — and that’s thanks to grassroots donors like you chipping in whatever you can. When I first launched this campaign, I knew there was a long road ahead of us. And I knew there was only one way I wanted to take this journey — with grassroots support from working people and families, not corporate donors. **Thanks to supporters like you, we’ve been able to stick to our values and run the type of campaign we can be proud of.** So before we head into the final stretch of this campaign, I just wanted to say thank you for your support so far, and I promise we’ll keep sticking to our values and putting people first as we push forward to November. name: Jason Yates, My Faith Votes
subject: An empty black robe
body:
Peter,
**I take no pleasure in saying this, but Chief Justice John Roberts is an empty black robe.**
This week, as he joined the Court’s 4 liberals in striking down Louisiana’s recently passed pro-life law, he’s shown that he’s not blind to politics and more interested in judicial activism than in reestablishing Constitutional government.
Perhaps the radical progressives don’t think we are paying attention, or that we don’t care enough to do anything about it.
**I’m here to tell you that the ambivalence and “hope for the best” disengagement of Christians across this nation must stop, and it must stop right now.**
Take a look at our country right now -- radical mobs loot our cities and deface our monuments, yet they remain silent about the roughly 19 million innocent black lives ended in the womb since _Roe v. Wade_ in 1973.
Corporate America and much of our political leadership is too weak to push back, so they echo empty slogans and celebrate “choice,” regardless of how destructive it is to our society and values.
**Well, what if the silent majority of Bible-believing Christians across this nation realized that we are bigger than the anarchist and Marxist mobs (we are), and that we, not they, will decide the future?**
What if, once our justices take the bench, they find the rightful recognition for upholding the Constitution and protecting human life to be more seductive than a few lines of Progressive praise in the _New York Times_?
[**Every day at My Faith Votes we are working to turn this vision into a reality, and after Chief Justice Roberts’ latest betrayal, I strongly urge you to join us in our work at My Faith Votes with your donation today**](https://secured.myfaithvotes.org/p200702apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200702apl01nd&n=1).
25 million Christians fail to vote in presidential elections, let alone all the other federal, state, and local elections. What in the world are we doing? Why are we being overrun and wondering why?
The solution to our problems lies with us. The Judeo-Christian values upon which this nation was founded were right then, they are right now, and they’ll be right tomorrow.
[**Please support My Faith Votes today, and help us to find these voters, motivate them, get them the information they need, and turn them out to the polls**](https://secured.myfaithvotes.org/p200702apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200702apl01nd&n=2).
Do not, for a single minute, think that we cannot turn this nation around. There is great hope, but there is much to be done.
[](https://secured.myfaithvotes.org/p200702apl01?e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200702apl01nd&n=3)
Thank you for everything you do.
Sincerely,
Jason Yates
CEO

clean_body: Peter, I take no pleasure in saying this, but Chief Justice John Roberts is an empty black robe. This week, as he joined the Court’s 4 liberals in striking down Louisiana’s recently passed pro-life law, he’s shown that he’s not blind to politics and more interested in judicial activism than in reestablishing Constitutional government. Perhaps the radical progressives don’t think we are paying attention, or that we don’t care enough to do anything about it. I’m here to tell you that the ambivalence and “hope for the best” disengagement of Christians across this nation must stop, and it must stop right now. Take a look at our country right now -- radical mobs loot our cities and deface our monuments, yet they remain silent about the roughly 19 million innocent black lives ended in the womb since Roe v. Wade in 1973. Corporate America and much of our political leadership is too weak to push back, so they echo empty slogans and celebrate “choice,” regardless of how destructive it is to our society and values. Well, what if the silent majority of Bible-believing Christians across this nation realized that we are bigger than the anarchist and Marxist mobs (we are), and that we, not they, will decide the future? What if, once our justices take the bench, they find the rightful recognition for upholding the Constitution and protecting human life to be more seductive than a few lines of Progressive praise in the New York Times? . 25 million Christians fail to vote in presidential elections, let alone all the other federal, state, and local elections. What in the world are we doing? Why are we being overrun and wondering why? The solution to our problems lies with us. The Judeo-Christian values upon which this nation was founded were right then, they are right now, and they’ll be right tomorrow. . Do not, for a single minute, think that we cannot turn this nation around. There is great hope, but there is much to be done. Thank you for everything you do. Sincerely, Jason Yates CEO ©2020 My Faith Votes 9422 Pikes Peak Way, Parker, CO 80138

name: Wesley Hunt
subject: The Story of America
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hunt for Congress
body:
Friend,

We’ve been given an incredible gift as Americans. The history of the United States is one of freedom, progress, hope, and taking the nation we inherited and passing it down to future generations better than we found it.

As we navigate this turbulent time in our history, we must remember that what unites us is stronger than what divides us.

The progress we have witnessed in our country was born of bravery, sacrifice, and an unwavering commitment to the words in our Declaration of Independence that “all men are created equal.” Our history, though imperfect, is worthy of reverence, and should inspire each and every one of us to strive boldly for a stronger, freer nation that lives up to our founding ideals.

In our newest digital advertisement, _The Story of America_ , released just before Independence Day, we remember that history and the strength of our union.

name: Michelle De La Isla
subject: I am humbled by your support
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MICHELLE FOR KANSAS
body:
I’m so excited to announce...
_Mayor De La Isla’s number one priority during this crisis is to slow the spread of COVID-19 in Kansas. In accordance with CDC guidelines, our campaign has canceled all in-person events, but we will continue engaging with voters and supporters online through email and social media. Thank you for reading and continuing to be part of our team._

Friends,

Let me say: thank you.

Earlier this week, we faced one of our biggest fundraising deadlines of this campaign -- our FEC end-of-quarter deadline. Hitting our goal was critical to ensuring this campaign had the resources necessary to take on whatever obstacles come our way.

**I am excited to share that thanks to the outpouring of support from folks like you, we reached our $60,000 digital fundraising goal!**

Look -- we have a real shot at finally giving Kansans the representation they deserve, but it won't be easy. The DCCC just named me a Red to Blue candidate, meaning this race is going to be one of the most competitive in the country. The only way I can get to Congress to lift up the voices of _all_ Kansans is with each and every one of you on my team.

We've accomplished so much together already, and I look forward to seeing what more we can do in the upcoming months.

Thanks for your ongoing support,

Michelle

**Michelle De La Isla is running for Congress to fight for families across KS-02. We understand that you may have other financial priorities during this challenging time. While we would greatly appreciate your support, please only contribute what you can.**

My opponent and her Far Left donors continue to outraise and outspend my campaign. Right now, our fundraising numbers simply can't keep up with their lies. With you by my side, I have faith that we will come out on top. Your support means the world to me.
All my best,
Susan Collins

The economy has added around 7.3 million jobs in the past two months. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6c9cd763e8&e=3ee4985eea)

| | |
# **Blowout: The U.S. Economy Added 4.8 Million Jobs in June**

“The economy has added around 7.3 million jobs in the past two months. The increase in the ranks of employed workers shows that companies ramped up hiring as the economy reopened and consumers came back to stores, restaurants, and other businesses that had been shuttered,” John Carney writes for _Breitbart_.

“The Trump administration’s aid programs appear to be working.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=898836cdf9&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is deploying a special federal unit across the country for the July 4 weekend in order to protect federal monuments and statues from a possible fresh wave of vandalism . . . The move marks an aggressive response by the Trump administration to push back against the violence and vandalism seen aimed at monuments in recent weeks,” Adam Shaw reports for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=28c8ba6d6a&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “Much too frequently of late, I have grieved with the family of an officer who took his or her own life. Our valiant police officers have one of the most perilous, stressful duties around, and from what I have seen in my nearly dozen years here, they do it with care, compassion and competence,” Archbishop Timothy Cardinal Dolan writes in the [_New York Post_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fae25ff063&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “Though we have faced unparalleled economic adversity in recent months, there remains light at the end of the tunnel thanks to President Trump’s United States, Mexico, Canada Agreement (USMCA) . . . Americans from all walks of life will benefit from a more favorable economic environment and increased job growth,” Lt. Governors Adam Gregg (IA), Jeanette Nuñez (FL), and Matt Pinnell (OK) write in [_The Hill_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e32052b5db&e=3ee4985eea).
---

name: Amanda Makki
subject: Amanda Makki Breaks $1 Million in Total Money Raised
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Amanda Makki For Congress Sincerely, Amanda Makki for Congress
body:
| _**Amanda Makki Breaks $1 Million in Total Money Raised**_ **St. Petersburg, FL –** Conservative Amanda Makki has surpassed $1 million in total contributions raised in her campaign to flip Florida’s 13th Congressional District, setting the stage for a head to head fight against Charlie Crist in November. In addition to her previous track record of strong financial support, Makki’s list of conservative endorsements and grassroot movement continues to flourish. Her recent endorsements come from esteemed military veterans including Senator Joni Ernst, the first female combat veteran ever elected to the United States Senate. Other honored veterans’ supporters include Congressmen Michael Waltz (Lt. Colonel), Congressman Neal Dunn (Major), and Combat veteran Robert O’Neill of the elite Navy SEAL team 6, who shot Osama bin Laden. _**“My team and I are grateful for all the support that our campaign has received. Over a year ago, we led the way to bring bold and effective conservative representation to Pinellas County and now we are full steam ahead,” said Makki. “For too long Charlie Crist hasn’t reflected the people of Pinellas. We must have leadership in Washington who supports our President, defends our Constitution, and fights against socialism. I am the only candidate in the nation who can take on radicals like Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib when I get to Congress.”**_ As the time-tested frontrunner candidate for Florida’s 13th Congressional District, Amanda Makki has also received the endorsements of House Republican Leader Kevin McCarthy, Republican Whip Steve Scalise, Health Care Ranking Member and Physician Congressman Michael Burgess and the former Sheriff for the City of Jacksonville Congressman John Rutherford. Locally, Amanda has the support of Seminole City Councilor Thom Barnhorn, Belleair Beach Mayor Joseph Manzo and Treasure Island Commissioner Tyler Payne. She was named to the National Republican Campaign Committee’s Young Guns program as one of only 27 top “Contender” candidates in the nation. Additionally, Amanda has received over 20 major endorsements including the Conservative groups FreedomWorks, Republican Main Street Partnership PAC, and Maverick PAC as well as groups supporting the strongest female candidates in the U.S. including VIEW PAC, Maggie’s List, and Rep. Elise Stefanik’s E‐PAC, that help female GOP candidates. She is also supported by the American College of Emergency Physicians and Associated Builders & Contractors Florida Gulf Coast Chapter. ### Paid for by Amanda Makki For Congress Sincerely, Amanda Makki for Congress
Republican Candidate for Florida’s 13th District
727-735-5921

For Online Contributions: [www.amandamakki.com](https://makki.nationbuilder.com/r?u=6MjyNB7aFByvrZ0SrqSVAVd-M-gWqrRQEisEX2TqEpDNH_FG_TSFx1cLErdscDQV&e=da3fb8759f1ee785466d5ec52a54a2bd&utm_source=makki&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=q2_press_release_thank_you&n=1)
For Contributions via Mail, please send to:
Amanda Makki for Congress

St. Petersburg, FL 33743

I am the only candidate in the nation who can take on radicals like Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib when I get to Congress.” As the time-tested frontrunner candidate for Florida’s 13th Congressional District, Amanda Makki has also received the endorsements of House Republican Leader Kevin McCarthy, Republican Whip Steve Scalise, Health Care Ranking Member and Physician Congressman Michael Burgess and the former Sheriff for the City of Jacksonville Congressman John Rutherford. Locally, Amanda has the support of Seminole City Councilor Thom Barnhorn, Belleair Beach Mayor Joseph Manzo and Treasure Island Commissioner Tyler Payne. She was named to the National Republican Campaign Committee’s Young Guns program as one of only 27 top “Contender” candidates in the nation. Additionally, Amanda has received over 20 major endorsements including the Conservative groups FreedomWorks, Republican Main Street Partnership PAC, and Maverick PAC as well as groups supporting the strongest female candidates in the U.S. including VIEW PAC, Maggie’s List, and Rep. Elise Stefanik’s E‐PAC, that help female GOP candidates. She is also supported by the American College of Emergency Physicians and Associated Builders & Contractors Florida Gulf Coast Chapter. ### Paid for by Amanda Makki For Congress Sincerely, Amanda Makki for Congress Republican Candidate for Florida’s 13th District 727-735-5921 For Online Contributions: For Contributions via Mail, please send to: Amanda Makki for Congress PO Box 47483 St. Petersburg, FL 33743 -=-=-

body:

---

---

| Protect Freedom PAC Launches Ad in Maine in Support of Eric Brakey
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001MtWw_M6f3fO3Dj3Y0Kj7v3wOFgBFd9X5tKiv1q99kupaw20mx3n4xdIMm1rWi6bCriWFN-otdL0lw49SszmQYIDUfiNWV6kB_aYDdMnWhGXHy47kO0nC-wkQlQhuVJ6BANSDbRNk5CC6ToBfqkhWLWSUmqMuiEInfEjocjp3OXkYbVqze7EAsxm9gJZ8vr7vHOINYpMfkSY=&c=mxI3nT_a4C0-0ylsF_20H4IQ0n1m_4YbyxMNhdzWzYMoJHCW4CiJCw==&ch=YEBwk75CWoqkHoVM5S_c3Va0oeNVpcALJFnj1qahEb3KuiCtBYENpA==)
---
| Athens, GA - Protect Freedom PAC, an independent organization dedicated to supporting pro-liberty and freedom-minded candidates, announced that they will be running an ad in support of Eric Brakey titled "Who's Behind It."
The ad, which launches today and will be a six-figure television buy in Maine, highlights Eric Brakey's commitment to support President Trump and to drain the swamp.
Protect Freedom PAC spokesman Michael Biundo stated:
"John Bolton lies about President Trump and now his henchmen are doing the exact same thing to Trump conservative Eric Brakey. They're scared of Brakey because they know he will stand with Trump, fight to drain the swamp, and advance an America First agenda. On the other hand, tax and spend RINOS Adrienne Bennett and Dale Crafts won't. They are part of the problem and can't be trusted. We're calling on the voters of ME-02 to ignore the Bolton lies and stand with Trump conservative Eric Brakey on Election Day."
The full ad can be viewed [here](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001MtWw_M6f3fO3Dj3Y0Kj7v3wOFgBFd9X5tKiv1q99kupaw20mx3n4xdIMm1rWi6bCriWFN-otdL0lw49SszmQYIDUfiNWV6kB_aYDdMnWhGXHy47kO0nC-wkQlQhuVJ6BANSDbRNk5CC6ToBfqkhWLWSUmqMuiEInfEjocjp3OXkYbVqze7EAsxm9gJZ8vr7vHOINYpMfkSY=&c=mxI3nT_a4C0-0ylsF_20H4IQ0n1m_4YbyxMNhdzWzYMoJHCW4CiJCw==&ch=YEBwk75CWoqkHoVM5S_c3Va0oeNVpcALJFnj1qahEb3KuiCtBYENpA==).
Protect Freedom PAC endorsed and supported candidates for statewide and federal office in a half dozen states in 2018 and looks to expand its reach for the 2020 cycle. The organization was founded by a team of veterans of the Donald Trump, Ron Paul, and Rand Paul campaigns for President.
---
| | |
---
| Protect Freedom PAC was founded for the purpose of supporting pro-freedom and liberty-minded candidates using the tactics, technology, and strategy that best fits each race individually. PFP was founded by seasoned political veterans with decades of national campaign experience, which includes working on the campaigns of both Ron and Rand Paul.

body:

| | I'm so grateful -- this team stepped up and pitched in and helped us reach our quarterly FEC fundraising goal. We can't slow down now. As we enter the last full quarter before Election Day, our race will become even more competitive. North Carolina will be a decisive battleground this November. That's why President Trump is planning to come back to our state for a rally and that's why Republicans are sending millions of dollars in dark money into North Carolina. I'm not taking a dime from corporate PACs because our fight is all about North Carolina -- not special interests. Our fight is about protecting voting rights and reproductive rights. It's about making health care affordable, investing in quality public education, and championing equality for all. It's about growing our economy and giving North Carolinians the tools and resources to succeed. Our fight is about flipping NC-06 for the first time in three decades, electing the first woman to ever represent our district, and spearheading the change we want to see in our community. And I can't do it without you. I didn’t see your name on our End of Quarter digital fundraising report, so I wanted to reach out and give you one more chance to contribute to our campaign. If you missed our fundraising emails and want to make a huge difference for my team, please chip in any amount using the link below.

Thank you for everything that you do.
Sincerely,

Nancy Mace

name: Holcomb Crew
subject: Back On Track: What’s next?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
We’ve seen increases in Indiana’s testing positivity rates, but the good news is that there are 200 testing sites across the state – so anyone can... | | |
---|---|---
Friends,

**"We have to be prepared. And we are." – Governor Holcomb**

It’s almost the 4th of July – and over this holiday weekend and beyond, Governor Holcomb is looking at COVID-19 data with Hoosiers’ health as his top priority.

That’s why he announced that, while a few restrictions will lift this weekend to allow for outdoor events – where the risk of transmission is up to 19 times less – _**the majority of Phase 4 will remain in place through at least July 17**_.

Here’s what we can now expect in Indiana for the next few weeks as part of the **[Back on Track plan](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=69e2757926de4160bf23248f836ee53f&_e=4m1E43G6OlgSYba15k_MTVyDkZyAB2WS_wcqhMtZn9Hf0ZKMX1cA18zHJtPP7PI2gH4DydCOAybkBiWqLkmlgnZsL_e8D0MxmcCHdwxkMCmoXNHYQSoPLVVqhFVYUhF8lcFREINVfyV7ltxFon3r2zzboRF6waUwuzvvd80HGuRoQ_-vBjdIpjRrFxEHxB7lFL-F4MyJ5mrj0dVbKPJJcgRBuL1sjE7luhAHSYaslRwo2kst-vDH8-esAIjr1GyZuzUnLKrN1P7zfetOxpSOvYitYgpaa6WPDYh379IoihasXxNZ1ZKFaiq6wtxjsn26lZtB0JloC3DGQJZA5I1Adb0Ct6KH_z1hg10DVLS4N4IHL5cMSFo_psB01pow4YY5)** –
* **Social Gatherings:** Following the CDC’s social distancing guidelines, gatherings will be limited to up to 250 people.
* **Eating Out:** Dining room food service may continue up to 75 percent capacity as long as social distancing is implemented.
* **Bars:** Bar seating in restaurants may continue operations at 50 percent capacity with social distancing.
* **Entertainment:** Cultural, entertainment, amusement parks, tourism sites and similar facilities may continue operations open at 50 percent capacity.

And that’s while _**fairs and festivals can move forward this summe****r**_ – as long as it’s done safely.

As always, state and local communities can impose stricter guidelines – and places like Elkhart County will remain in Stage 4 until at least July 13.

Why the delay? Governor Holcomb has always said this will be a data-driven decision, and here’s where the data currently stands:
[ ](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=69e2757926de4160bf23248f836ee53f&_e=4m1E43G6OlgSYba15k_MTVyDkZyAB2WS_wcqhMtZn9Hf0ZKMX1cA18zHJtPP7PI2gH4DydCOAybkBiWqLkmlgnZsL_e8D0MxmcCHdwxkMCmoXNHYQSoPLVVqhFVYUhF8lcFREINVfyV7ltxFon3r2zzboRF6waUwuzvvd80HGuRng28qZ4LO29EtreCaFf5Qjd3hmR5LJauQjf-PP0rd3omhFALpqIqUOlesTBKseQXN78jkkJFluderkbUTmJZOSZLRjw7lUIy1EF4XywWf1vrRSrMLLl5_yP1d7H4a0sote2Oa3lAIzafO7f7AO-KDcoxMe7HqiRxiDacjE3qmVBBfBuO-lPrJgla7AhHIbCe7RWa50FGkHhQZSMBC4WoN)
* **Hospitalizations** – As you can see from the chart above, _**Indiana is seeing a slight uptick in our hospitalization rate**_. While the uptick isn’t as strong as a few other states – it’s still an important change to watch.
* **Surge Capacity** – Indiana’s hospital surge capacity still remains strong, with 40% of ICU beds available and 83% of ventilators available.
* **Testing** – We’ve seen _**increases in Indiana’s testing positivity rates**_ , but the good news is that there are **[200 testing sites](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=69e2757926de4160bf23248f836ee53f&_e=4m1E43G6OlgSYba15k_MTRnzCyKADjSxo55RPYCHhFCvxWs4IVZsiTkh7NOutseAHtLWzNia2PKpzDF9cKwLNSXNllSHiKqSZMupaj0wCjUvDw-GIp-wQPurl5oVzL8o5CPVj_SvyJtBfHRuhsqLu6P271vl5LgHARyq3KkcQPFSI9EvSkr6YOgCIm5UKCxb195kYQFedkfgC6aCSW5w6ZNo8vR8OnKtkJgGHD8yXDWlppj3_6UfPUd6mhmsA1HPt3y3G3Dos3XyXc0NNwAO5USptf9gu4VSYoF2_7CM97ZL_c5EEJ2pJ_vXDQChSXV53fIh202ENQoCRbSZj28hb0LppzV_sk0Nq9l5Pr-cK0MvCd3FeG_0232Ud5IylvItNLz0AYTX5ku8ch4c7kMTkA%3D%3D)** across the state – so anyone can now get tested (and for free at state-sponsored sites).
* **Contact Tracing** – Indiana’s contact tracing team is _**making 2,500 daily calls**_ to people who have tested positive or their close contacts.

This Back on Track plan might take just a little bit longer – but remember: _**Indiana is making progress. And it’s all while Hoosiers’ health comes first**_.

So as you’re out and about this Independence Day weekend, please remember to be safe. Practice social distancing, wear your mask, and let’s look out for our fellow Hoosiers!

Happy (early) 4th,
-Your Holcomb Crew

P.S. Need a reason to mask up as you’re going out? **[Watch this video](http://links.holcombforindiana.com/u/click?_t=60bbc6d769fe4010b248f63c6cb0e872&_m=69e2757926de4160bf23248f836ee53f&_e=4m1E43G6OlgSYba15k_MTVXMw5qnjmrGqTf5T-KWM4LFwixWJWtmm0C2N75Zry4U555VYq4Yz7orY166d2cF_WColblWjLvClux9utNiXG5oDLPYcPVlyMMEMsCGJ_3WLcWS04ZzbBOToBly7uewCnrIwzByThW6_RzN5R3y9PnoChoRNLyPa8dGJ-fCE-xzWNNrdmJ3McLgWGxJv7pYRwqTTZJb_0Pus8XmO7TsOUJwyWxVAARkf76ra4FHhQPo02gTDYlgj9Ag3-wuejOcDfA4JQzrFPusTZy-lJ5pKsF2vqo7-V5aGoPSths6MOHB37XRYiImO6Rbz_6YULrVt9noraaYCxqSePlVDzvWyDwRbgN1nUgFWAKkbfqkhGR2i3ze6cipFkkRO2wZJ5IKXQ%3D%3D)** and then tell us on social media why you #MaskUpHoosiers.

| | Team, we’ve got bad news.

President Trump just nominated an unqualified, hyper-partisan head to the Bureau of Land Management. It’s clear that he has no respect for our public lands—just look at what the Trump Administration has been doing under his watch:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/18119684/217300762/-32929971?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NWQyNTNkMS1jMGJjLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BG9Y5OSaAZ8LfEqbhX_neXeo7fyMdGbxNx3W50KIfuA=&emci=731672a7-a5bc-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=55d253d1-c0bc-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=1295540)
The Trump Administration thinks that it owns America’s public lands. But that couldn’t be further from the truth.

[America’s public lands belong to ALL of us—not special interests in Washington.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/18119685/217300763/-32929971?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NWQyNTNkMS1jMGJjLWVhMTEtOWIwNS0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BG9Y5OSaAZ8LfEqbhX_neXeo7fyMdGbxNx3W50KIfuA=&emci=731672a7-a5bc-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&emdi=55d253d1-c0bc-ea11-9b05-00155d039e74&ceid=1295540)

In Colorado, public lands are part of our history, culture, and economy. That’s why Michael is fighting against this incompetent nominee and the Trump Administration’s blatant disregard for our public lands, but he can’t do it alone.

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Wear a mask this weekend
body:
Your RI COVID-19 News Update
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
Like you, I am so excited for the Fourth of July. Celebrations will be different this year, But it's important now more than ever that we reflect on what unites us as Americans.
It is also going to be more important than ever that we wear our masks this weekend. Wearing a mask still feels a bit strange, but it's also an opportunity for self-expression. It's a way to say to your fellow Rhode Islanders: I'm looking out for you. I wanted to take a second today to [show you some of my favorite masks](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Un1b0WZo4vhXSOncmzoM6O-QJykrADRXrws_cuVa0Zg5btVfWrQQpTpLkUGK0vgEk7VodzPA_WGTEWFRAx-SIrEYGblT8VvMjpF2YRCsqgpy6O2YaB6r_fwB-pIjIAd8bKTnrJz2LshNlQnX1wLl4g==&c=rDRXV9O-ABqL2uu_B5r8w4wstifKiFNQ7RVaDW0PeG_19rJsO6Pm1g==&ch=J7I91D6lQYhOiYzMKagwHRuCu-d-MvYXpIQNSD_cwx_xI0U6FXmZvA==) that I wear.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Un1b0WZo4vhXSOncmzoM6O-QJykrADRXrws_cuVa0Zg5btVfWrQQpTpLkUGK0vgEk7VodzPA_WGTEWFRAx-SIrEYGblT8VvMjpF2YRCsqgpy6O2YaB6r_fwB-pIjIAd8bKTnrJz2LshNlQnX1wLl4g==&c=rDRXV9O-ABqL2uu_B5r8w4wstifKiFNQ7RVaDW0PeG_19rJsO6Pm1g==&ch=J7I91D6lQYhOiYzMKagwHRuCu-d-MvYXpIQNSD_cwx_xI0U6FXmZvA==)
---
| I hope that you've got a collection of masks that you love, too! I'd love to see yours, too. I'm challenging all Rhode Islanders to show me your Rhode Island pride. Take a photo of yourself wearing a mask and share it using #MaskUpRI. You can share them with me by [email](mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov?Subject=Here%27s%20my%20favorite%20mask), on [Twitter](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Un1b0WZo4vhXSOncmzoM6O-QJykrADRXrws_cuVa0Zg5btVfWrQQpTpLkUGK0vgElWoj8QPMcHlVZAe4mkmH_dbsYDIsDcBiRbL9ZTtVIEGc7n3H8dwyn_Xkbv9qReA0IVucRp9D2DJaF5oFdBCVlFnCMtxu9O98IrU5QOEH1P--jGsU03GKBv8Pw5XVCbe9XltGnd5MiFdgnjo5D4hl-SK84v0CxS_E0t4Z6n1_N1jYYqILN9URCUVJ6h0gt0AMvWHYFQ7WO55aPptcJux16c0bVAvISamw&c=rDRXV9O-ABqL2uu_B5r8w4wstifKiFNQ7RVaDW0PeG_19rJsO6Pm1g==&ch=J7I91D6lQYhOiYzMKagwHRuCu-d-MvYXpIQNSD_cwx_xI0U6FXmZvA==), or on [Facebook](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Un1b0WZo4vhXSOncmzoM6O-QJykrADRXrws_cuVa0Zg5btVfWrQQpSVS3t1fu2FUoY5cHUjYGsIy2quh-Y9m4MNFKCSlpKEkcW1kmwJqJiLvO_QEgX4PmcO2FzUjiH0Bc_QBpNNdnStNGT8ZcUMNSFw6gF5w27b0r6P_vPw4dTY=&c=rDRXV9O-ABqL2uu_B5r8w4wstifKiFNQ7RVaDW0PeG_19rJsO6Pm1g==&ch=J7I91D6lQYhOiYzMKagwHRuCu-d-MvYXpIQNSD_cwx_xI0U6FXmZvA==). I can't wait to see how you're styling your mask.
Of course, there are so many other important health guidelines that we need to follow this weekend. Please, make sure that you are:

* Washing your hands often throughout the day, using warm water and soap. If soap and water are not available, using hand sanitizer with at least 60% alcohol.
* Coughing or sneezing into your elbow.
* Avoiding touching your eyes, nose, or mouth.
* Staying home and do not leave your house if you are sick, unless it is for emergency medical care.

Right now, we're staying ahead of the rest of the country as cases spike. We can only keep it that way if we all work together and follow the rules to keep things safe.

| Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's [COVID-19 data dashboard](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001Un1b0WZo4vhXSOncmzoM6O-QJykrADRXrws_cuVa0Zg5btVfWrQQpWFB3ulOpJ5eAROfQ7BCN4gI-0ebDocL_RKPEDz-v8O3XedEhJ8HtetRo-STeqigW8swsd0OsEYeLrDVYVIG3GpaMmy1Rme3PP0g7pu4g-H4wWjJzRaTHtEEDtbT-Oo4mCP8eSTQmBsUHrkz3ueX14A=&c=rDRXV9O-ABqL2uu_B5r8w4wstifKiFNQ7RVaDW0PeG_19rJsO6Pm1g==&ch=J7I91D6lQYhOiYzMKagwHRuCu-d-MvYXpIQNSD_cwx_xI0U6FXmZvA==).
---
| Please, be safe this holiday weekend. We've come so far together--we can't let up now. If you can wear a mask, putting it on is a great way to help keep your community safe. Remember that your mask is for protecting the people around you. I'm asking all Rhode Islanders to be good neighbors.

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: NEW: June jobs report SHATTERS expectations
body:
America added 4.8 million jobs in June—the largest monthly increase ever recorded, according to today’s report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=10044285fa&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 2, 2020
---
| | |
# **NEW: June jobs report SHATTERS expectations**

**America added 4.8 million jobs** in June—the largest monthly increase ever recorded, according to today’s report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

**With 7.5 million jobs** added over the past two months, America’s economic comeback from the Coronavirus is taking off well ahead of schedule.

🎬 [**President Trump: Today’s report is “spectacular news” for America**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2134180c2d&e=3ee4985eea)

**“There’s not been anything like this** —record setting,” President Trump said at a press briefing this morning. “We’ve implemented an aggressive strategy to vanquish and kill the virus, and protect Americans at the highest risk, while allowing those at lower risk to return safely to work. That’s what’s happening.”

**After May and June** ranked as the two largest monthly jobs gains in history, an estimated one-third of all job losses from March and April have now been recovered.

**“Our work won't be done** until every single American who lost their job because of COVID gets back to work,” Treasury Secretary Steven Mnuchin said today.

**June’s job gains** were spread broadly across American industries, with the hard-hit leisure and hospitality sector seeing the biggest turnaround:
* 2.1 million leisure & hospitality jobs

* 740,000 retail jobs

* 568,000 education & healthcare jobs

* 357,000 service jobs

* 356,000 manufacturing jobs

**The Great American Comeback** is reducing unemployment for a number of historically marginalized groups, too. African-American workers saw historic gains with more than 400,000 jobs added last month. Hispanic-American employment is up by 1.5 million, and the unemployment rate for women fell even quicker than the rate for men.

**On top of that** , “workers with a high school education or less made the biggest strides of all,” President Trump said.

**There is more work** to do in the months ahead as we rebuild the strongest economy on Earth together. The incredible, expectations-busting jobs reports in May and June, however, should give every American hope that we’re heading toward a bright future.

🎬 [**President Trump: Stock market is soaring with best gains in 20 years**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a3e7e32d66&e=3ee4985eea)

**READ** : [**A Record 4.8 Million Jobs Created in June**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a69c17fdaa&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **President Trump hosts ‘Spirit of America’ showcase!**

**President Trump** welcomed small business leaders to the White House today to spotlight their incredible work as America reopens from the Coronavirus pandemic.

**“The small businesses** represented in this room continue a great and noble American heritage,” he said. “You’re entrepreneurs, artisans, creators, craftsman who forge your own path, made your own products, and provide good-paying jobs for our citizens.”

**Eighty percent** of U.S. small businesses are now open, and new business applications have doubled since March. Thanks to President Trump’s Paycheck Protection Program, many American workers have stayed on the payroll during the pandemic, lifting incomes and helping to spark a quicker economic comeback.

🎬** **[**President Trump: 80% of small businesses are now open**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3452d8b8bd&e=3ee4985eea)
---
